_meta.note: This file is the live data store for FlyScout. Entries with auto_detected:false are bootstrap/seed examples included to demonstrate the schema before the first automated scan runs. Entries with auto_detected:true are written exclusively by check_rules.py via the scheduled GitHub Action — do not hand-edit those going forward. Seed entries should be verified and either confirmed or removed once real monitoring is live.

changes.title: EU Interchange Fee Regulation caps in force
changes.summary: EU Regulation 2015/751 caps consumer debit card interchange at 0.2% and consumer credit card interchange at 0.3% of transaction value for both Visa and Mastercard. Commercial/corporate cards are exempt from the cap.
changes.new_snippet: Interchange fees for consumer debit cards shall not exceed 0.2% and for consumer credit cards shall not exceed 0.3% of the value of the transaction.

changes.title: UK-EEA cross-border card-not-present interchange raised post-Brexit
changes.summary: Following the UK's exit from the EU's Interchange Fee Regulation framework, Visa and Mastercard raised interchange fees on consumer card transactions between UK merchants and EEA-issued cards (and vice versa) for card-not-present transactions, citing the cross-border exemption no longer applying. This was widely reported by UK media and merchant bodies as a substantial cost increase for cross-border e-commerce.
changes.new_value: Increased — exact current rate should be verified against current Visa/Mastercard UK bulletins
changes.new_snippet: Cross-border UK-EEA interchange no longer subject to the EU IFR cap following the end of the Brexit transition period.

changes.title: RBA card payments and surcharging review (open consultation)
changes.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia opened a review of card payment costs, including surcharging practices, with options under consideration ranging from tighter enforcement of the 'cost of acceptance' surcharge cap to a broader prohibition on surcharging for some payment types. This is an open regulatory process, not yet a finalised rule change.
changes.source_name: RBA Review of Merchant Card Payment Costs and Surcharging
changes.new_snippet: The Reserve Bank is reviewing card payment costs and surcharging practices, with a consultation paper published for industry feedback.
